title: SOS Task Completed Message
description: Event emitted by an agent when it finishes a task (successfully or otherwise). Triggers feedback loop, settlement, and ledger entry on the SOS bus.

type.description: Discriminator field identifying this as a task.completed event.

source.description: Bus identity of the agent that completed the task. Must match the agent: URI scheme followed by a lowercase, dash-allowed agent name.

target.description: Destination channel or agent on the SOS bus. Defaults to the shared tasks channel.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when this message was emitted.

version.description: SemVer (MAJOR.MINOR or MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) of the message schema this record conforms to.

message_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this message instance. Used for deduplication and tracing.

payload.description: Task-specific result data including outcome, timing, resource usage, and optional error detail.

task_id.description: Identifier of the task that was completed. Must be alphanumeric with underscores and dashes.

status.description: Terminal status of the task. 'done' = success; 'failed' = agent error; 'cancelled' = externally aborted; 'timeout' = exceeded deadline.

completed_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the task reached its terminal state.

result.description: Arbitrary structured output produced by the task. Present only when status is 'done'. Schema is task-type-specific.

duration_s.description: Wall-clock duration of the task in whole seconds from start to completion.

artifacts.description: Opaque references (URLs, file paths, or object keys) to durable outputs produced by the task.

items.description: A single artifact reference — URL, absolute path, or opaque storage key.

tokens_spent.description: Total LLM tokens consumed by the agent during this task (input + output + cache read). Used for ledger and quota tracking.

cost_cents.description: Computed cost of the task in US cents (integer, no fractional cents). Used for settlement and billing.

error.description: Structured error detail. Should be present when status is 'failed', 'cancelled', or 'timeout'. Omit for 'done'.

code.description: SOS error code in the format SOS-XXXX (e.g. SOS-4001). Maps to the canonical SOS error registry.

message.description: Human-readable description of the error suitable for logging and operator review.

traceback.description: Full stack trace or exception chain as a plain string. Optional — include when available for post-mortem debugging.
